title: eragrostis pilosa var. capillaris
intro: Eragrostis pilosa var. capillaris, also known as the hairy love grass or creeping love grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eragrostis pilosa var. capillaris include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a raw material for teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
